answer.summary: Arsenal Football Club has launched and operates multiple digital products, platforms, and fan experiences. The most significant is 'The Arsenal' app, a content-first digital platform launched in early March 2026 (developed over 3+ years with NTT DATA as Official Digital Transformation Partner), which consolidates original programming, personalised news feeds, a Match Centre with live coverage, a Ticketing Hub, retail/merch drops, and exclusive competitions into a single free iOS/Android app. Original series include 'The Link Up' (Bukayo Saka cultural crossover), 'The Big Match' (fan dating show), 'Modern Leader' (Martin Ødegaard documentary), 'Raising The Bar' (Declan Rice & Thierry Henry), 'Gunner Girls', and 'Only in the Land' (Arsenal Women's Champions League documentary). Prior to this, Arsenal launched 'My Arsenal Rewards' in August 2021 — the first UK Premier League loyalty programme — offering points for match attendance, ticket purchases, and digital engagement, redeemable for exclusive merchandise, VIP experiences, and prize draws, with a Barclays-issued prepaid membership card also serving as stadium access. Arsenal also issues the Arsenal Fan Token (AFC) on the Chiliz blockchain via Socios.com, giving holders governance-lite voting rights on club decisions plus access to exclusive content and VIP experiences. Arsenal.com serves as a Webby Award-winning fan engagement platform with free Digital Membership for global supporters, and 'Live from N5' is a live matchday show broadcast from Emirates Stadium for men's home fixtures.

Collectively these roles reveal Arsenal is transitioning from a traditional football club commercial model to a data-first, digitally native global sports-entertainment business, with heavy emphasis on personalising supporter experiences, scaling partnership revenue, and AI-enabled content delivery.

answer.summary: Arsenal Football Club has launched and operates multiple digital products, platforms, and fan experiences. The most significant is 'The Arsenal' app, a content-first digital platform launched in early March 2026 (developed over 3+ years with NTT DATA as Official Digital Transformation Partner), which consolidates original programming, personalised news feeds, a Match Centre with live coverage, a Ticketing Hub, retail/merch drops, and exclusive competitions into a single free iOS/Android app. Original series include 'The Link Up' (Bukayo Saka cultural crossover), 'The Big Match' (fan dating show), 'Modern Leader' (Martin Ødegaard documentary), 'Raising The Bar' (Declan Rice & Thierry Henry), 'Gunner Girls', and 'Only in the Land' (Arsenal Women's Champions League documentary). Prior to this, Arsenal launched 'My Arsenal Rewards' in August 2021 — the first UK Premier League loyalty programme — offering points for match attendance, ticket purchases, and digital engagement, redeemable for exclusive merchandise, VIP experiences, and prize draws, with a Barclays-issued prepaid membership card also serving as stadium access. Arsenal also issues the Arsenal Fan Token (AFC) on the Chiliz blockchain via Socios.com, giving holders governance-lite voting rights on club decisions plus access to exclusive content and VIP experiences. Arsenal.com serves as a Webby Award-winning fan engagement platform with free Digital Membership for global supporters, and 'Live from N5' is a live matchday show broadcast from Emirates Stadium for men's home fixtures.

answer.summary: Arsenal Football Club has launched and operates multiple digital products, platforms, and fan experiences. The most significant is 'The Arsenal' app, a content-first digital platform launched in early March 2026 (developed over 3+ years with NTT DATA as Official Digital Transformation Partner), which consolidates original programming, personalised news feeds, a Match Centre with live coverage, a Ticketing Hub, retail/merch drops, and exclusive competitions into a single free iOS/Android app. Original series include 'The Link Up' (Bukayo Saka cultural crossover), 'The Big Match' (fan dating show), 'Modern Leader' (Martin Ødegaard documentary), 'Raising The Bar' (Declan Rice & Thierry Henry), 'Gunner Girls', and 'Only in the Land' (Arsenal Women's Champions League documentary). Prior to this, Arsenal launched 'My Arsenal Rewards' in August 2021 — the first UK Premier League loyalty programme — offering points for match attendance, ticket purchases, and digital engagement, redeemable for exclusive merchandise, VIP experiences, and prize draws, with a Barclays-issued prepaid membership card also serving as stadium access. Arsenal also issues the Arsenal Fan Token (AFC) on the Chiliz blockchain via Socios.com, giving holders governance-lite voting rights on club decisions plus access to exclusive content and VIP experiences. Arsenal.com serves as a Webby Award-winning fan engagement platform with free Digital Membership for global supporters, and 'Live from N5' is a live matchday show broadcast from Emirates Stadium for men's home fixtures.
